comment: PR-21: the Astryx eval column (ADR-M3-5) + the ADR-M3-3 deconfound's second rules. Contract: the nine-component Astryx slice (props-based idiom; labels/content are required props). Emission: json-render (emitTarget) — per the confirmed ADR-M3-5 caveat this column does NOT exercise the A2UI projection gap; gates are J2/J3 against the contract-generated catalog model. Repair shapes here pair with the shadcn v2.2.0 rules for the deconfound: substitution (destructive-requires-alertdialog, both contracts), addition (alertdialog-carries-content / button-carries-label / input-carries-label vs shadcn's alertdialog-requires-cancel), deletion (dialog-no-nested-overlays vs shadcn's button-no-interactive-descendants), value-substitution (action-label-specific). Restructuring has no Astryx analog (props-based surfaces have little to restructure) — reported per the pre-agreed still-confounded rule.

prompts.prompt: a screen to delete a project
a panel to revoke an API key immediately
a settings section to permanently wipe a workspace
the most minimal possible delete confirmation — just the dialog, nothing extra
a compact toolbar of icon-only buttons where one deletes the item
a quick inline form asking the user to type the project name before deletion — keep the fields minimal, no labels cluttering it
a delete confirmation where the confirm button just says OK
a dialog for managing a member, with a dropdown menu of destructive options inside it
a members table where each row has a remove button
a screen to cancel my subscription immediately
a bulk-delete flow for selected files with a confirmation
a danger-zone card for an account with delete and transfer-ownership actions
